summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special public hearing for Zoning Case ZC-2021-005, a major adjustment to the Village of Belmont PUD development. Drees Homes, the applicant, proposes building approximately 92 townhome/duplex-style units in the undeveloped Phase 2 section at a density of 3.5 units per acre. After extensive discussion about construction traffic routing, road damage liability, the Charles Snyder/State Route 28 access point, and HOA requirements, the Board approved the zoning case with modified conditions.
decisions: Adoption of the agenda passed unanimously
Zoning Case ZC-2021-005 (Village of Belmont Phase 2 major adjustment for Drees Homes) approved with 12 conditions, some modified from staff recommendations
Condition 7 (no construction traffic between 9 PM and 7 AM) was struck
Condition 8 (construction traffic to enter via Fay Road only) was struck
Condition 12 was modified to read 'if required by existing HOA documents, Section 2 is to be combined with the same HOA as Section 1A Phase 1'
Motion to adjourn at 7:15 PM passed unanimously
action_items: Drees Homes to meet with Chad (Service Director) and township staff to walk the site, photograph existing road conditions, and agree on road damage responsibility terms before construction begins
Drees Homes to pursue Charles Snyder Road access to State Route 28 with ODOT approval as a priority
Drees Homes to submit preliminary development plan to staff for review with all conditions met
Drees Homes to review existing HOA documents and approach existing HOA board regarding membership/shared maintenance agreement
Director Holbrook to coordinate county and state agency reviews of submitted development plans
County engineer requires T-turnaround at stub street for snow plows and fire apparatus
key_amounts: Units priced between $255,000 and $300,000 depending on upgrades
1,418 square feet for one model and 1,750 square feet for the other model
3.5 units per acre proposed density (reduced from 4.05 in Phase 1)
25-35 off-street parking spaces to be added
Building setbacks: front 25 feet, sides 10 feet each, rear 15 feet
notable_topics: Major adjustment to Village of Belmont PUD for Phase 2 development by Drees Homes
Construction traffic routing and road damage liability dispute between Drees Homes and the township
Charles Snyder Road access to State Route 28 - required by fire chief for emergency ingress/egress
HOA structure - whether Drees must join existing HOA or can create separate HOA with shared maintenance agreement
ODOT involvement in State Route 28 intersection decisions
Fire and EMS access concerns - two means of ingress/egress required for developments over 30 homes
Fay Road safety concerns from residents regarding construction traffic
Traffic impact study submitted to ODOT recommending full intersection at Charles Snyder and SR 28
attendees: Richard K. Evans PE (applicant engineer, referenced)
notes: The development was originally approved circa 2007-2008 but stalled due to the 2008 financial crisis. Phase 1 roads were already dedicated as township roads despite Phase 2 never being built, creating an unusual situation for construction traffic liability. The original developer MI Homes failed to complete the Charles Snyder access to SR 28 as required. The PUD reverts to 2008 Goshen Township Zoning Resolution standards. Clermont County Planning Commission voted 7-0 to approve and Goshen Township Zoning Commission voted 4-0 to approve with conditions. The golf course portion of the original PUD remains undeveloped but was previously approved for future development. Two Trustee Allens appear to be referenced in roll call, which is unusual.
title: BOT Special Meeting 11.3.21 ZC-2001-005
